design_philosophy.core_loop: Automatically descend a path while avoiding obstacles and collecting card suit orbs, racing against a lit bomb fuse to reach the detonation point in time
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (bomb timer) vs. obstacle avoidance; perfectionism requirement (collect all 4 orbs on first run for best ending)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Slapstick hero preventing explosions through reflexive navigation; comedic platforming with emphasis on animation and gags
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavily punishment-based design philosophy: requires flawless first-run collection of all card orbs to unlock true endings; multiple collision penalties slow progress; no room for error on intended path to perfect completion. The game is intentionally demanding and gatekeeps content behind perfect play requirements, creating a 'memorization then execution' paradigm unusual for platformers of its era

difficulty.lives_system: Collision-based health; multiple hits in a stage cause level loss
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system exists; using continues disqualifies player from best ending (locks out 'Good Ending')
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with no mid-stage checkpoints; failure restarts entire stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal. Game is on-rails (auto-descending path); player controls navigation around fixed obstacles. Difficulty is entirely determined by obstacle placement and bomb timer tuning. No difficulty settings documented. Hard mode exists but requires same perfect collection demands as normal mode

progression.unlock_mechanism: Collecting all 4 card suit orbs per stage unlocks Honey heart; collecting all 5 Honey hearts unlocks 'Good Ending'; final stage requires correct lock selection from 10 locks to complete rescue
replayability_hooks: Three distinct endings based on collection perfection and continue usage
Hard mode with same collection requirements for alternate challenge
Hidden level select accessible via controller code (L+R both controllers + button combinations)
Hidden sound test unlocked after beating game
Game Boy Advance re-release added 11 new levels and 4 unlockable mini-games with multiplayer

world_building_techniques: Character-driven setup: Kid Klown must rescue Princess Honey from villain Black Jack/Dirty Joe
Ending variation reflects narrative success (rescued vs. not rescued)
Slapstick visual comedy through animations and character interactions rather than dialogue

faction_indicators: Hero vs. Villain dynamic: Kid Klown (protagonist) vs. Black Jack/Dirty Joe (antagonist)
danger_signals: Lit bomb fuse as universal threat across all stages
environmental_storytelling: Isometric perspective creates sense of descent/pursuit through varied stage environments
Bomb mechanics and fuse imagery establish Black Jack's threat
Stage progression moves toward rescue climax

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Kid Klown in Crazy Chase
Wikipedia - Kid Klown in Crazy Chase
